overview.title: ESP32 & ESP8266 Resources
overview.description: Comprehensive guide to ESP32 and ESP8266 WiFi/Bluetooth microcontrollers for IoT projects, including development boards, modules, and specifications

boards.name: ESP32 DevKit V1 (DOIT)
boards.shortDesc: Popular 30-pin ESP32 development board with WiFi, Bluetooth, dual-core 240MHz processor
boards.fullDescription: The ESP32 DevKit V1 DOIT is one of the most popular ESP32 development boards featuring the ESP32-WROOM-32 module. It offers dual-core processing up to 240MHz, integrated WiFi and Bluetooth, and a rich set of peripherals. Compatible with Arduino IDE and easily programmable via USB.

specifications.Processor: Xtensa dual-core 32-bit LX6, up to 240 MHz
specifications.Connectivity: WiFi 802.11 b/g/n, Bluetooth v4.2 (BR/EDR and BLE)
specifications.Power: 5V via Micro-USB or VIN, 3.3V regulated output

pinout.Note: GPIO6-11 reserved for flash, GPIO34-39 input-only

codeSnippet.title: WiFi Scanner Example

boards.name: ESP32-C3 SuperMini
boards.shortDesc: Ultra-compact RISC-V ESP32-C3 board with WiFi 4, Bluetooth 5.0, USB-C, only 22.5 x 18mm
boards.fullDescription: The ESP32-C3 SuperMini is an ultra-compact development board featuring a 32-bit RISC-V single-core processor. Despite its tiny size, it packs WiFi and Bluetooth 5.0 connectivity, making it perfect for battery-powered IoT projects and wearables where space is critical.

specifications.Security: AES-128/256, RSA, HMAC, SHA, secure boot

codeSnippet.title: Blink LED Example

boards.name: ESP32-S3 DevKitC
boards.shortDesc: High-performance dual-core LX7 with AI acceleration, WiFi 4, Bluetooth 5.0 for advanced IoT
boards.fullDescription: The ESP32-S3 DevKitC features a powerful dual-core Xtensa LX7 processor running at 240MHz with AI acceleration capabilities. Designed for advanced IoT applications including voice recognition, image processing, and machine learning at the edge.

specifications.Processor: Dual-core 32-bit Xtensa LX7, up to 240 MHz
specifications.Special Features: Vector instructions for AI, LCD interface, DVP camera interface

codeSnippet.title: Bluetooth Service Example

boards.name: ESP32-CAM (AI-Thinker)
boards.shortDesc: ESP32 with OV2640 2MP camera, microSD slot, WiFi/BT for video streaming and image processing
boards.fullDescription: The ESP32-CAM combines an ESP32 dual-core microcontroller with a 2-megapixel OV2640 camera module and microSD card slot. Perfect for video streaming, surveillance, facial recognition, and image-based IoT applications. Requires external FTDI programmer for uploading code.

specifications.Processor: Dual-core Tensilica LX6, up to 240 MHz

pinout.Note: Limited GPIO due to camera/SD interfaces

codeSnippet.title: Camera Web Server

boards.name: ESP8266 NodeMCU V3
boards.shortDesc: Popular ESP8266-12E dev board with WiFi, Arduino IDE compatible, CP2102/CH340 USB-UART
boards.fullDescription: The NodeMCU ESP8266 is one of the most popular open-source development boards for IoT applications. Based on the ESP8266-12E module with integrated 2.4GHz WiFi, it's breadboard-friendly, Arduino IDE compatible, and features a built-in USB-to-serial converter for easy programming.

specifications.Power: 5V via Micro-USB or VIN, 3.3V operating voltage

codeSnippet.title: Web Server Example

boards.name: Wemos D1 Mini ESP8266
boards.shortDesc: Ultra-compact ESP8266 board, perfect for battery-powered projects, 34 x 25mm size
boards.fullDescription: The Wemos D1 Mini is an ultra-compact ESP8266 development board favored for its small size and low power consumption. With all the power of the ESP8266-12E/F in a tiny form factor, it's ideal for battery-powered IoT projects and space-constrained applications.

specifications.Power: 5V via Micro-USB, up to 24V max input, 3.3V logic

codeSnippet.title: Deep Sleep Battery Example

boards.name: Adafruit Feather HUZZAH ESP8266
boards.shortDesc: Feather form-factor ESP8266 with LiPo battery support, JST connector, on-board charging
boards.fullDescription: The Adafruit Feather HUZZAH ESP8266 combines the WiFi power of ESP8266 with Adafruit's Feather ecosystem. Features include automatic LiPo battery charging, JST connector for battery, and compatibility with all Feather Wings for easy expansion. Perfect for portable WiFi projects.

codeSnippet.title: Battery Monitoring Example

boards.name: ESP32-C6 DevKit
boards.shortDesc: RISC-V ESP32 with WiFi 6, Bluetooth 5.3, and Matter support for next-gen smart home
boards.fullDescription: The ESP32-C6 is Espressif's first WiFi 6 SoC featuring a 32-bit RISC-V processor at 160MHz. It integrates 2.4GHz WiFi 6 (802.11ax), Bluetooth 5.3 LE, and IEEE 802.15.4 for Thread and Zigbee, making it perfect for Matter-compatible smart home devices with improved efficiency and low latency.

specifications.Communication: 3x UART, 2x I2C, 3x SPI, I2S, USB Serial/JTAG
specifications.Security: RSA-3072 secure boot, AES-128/256 encryption, HMAC, ECDSA

codeSnippet.title: WiFi 6 Connection Example

boards.name: ESP32-H2 DevKit
boards.shortDesc: Ultra-low-power ESP32 with Zigbee, Thread, and Bluetooth 5 LE for mesh networking
boards.fullDescription: The ESP32-H2 is designed specifically for ultra-low-power IoT mesh networking. Featuring a 32-bit RISC-V processor at 96MHz, it supports IEEE 802.15.4 (Zigbee/Thread) and Bluetooth 5 LE, making it ideal for battery-powered smart home devices and mesh network applications.

codeSnippet.title: Zigbee Device Example

boards.name: ESP32-P4 Function Evaluation Board
boards.shortDesc: High-performance dual-core RISC-V at 400MHz for edge AI, HMI, and industrial IoT
boards.fullDescription: The ESP32-P4 is Espressif's most powerful MCU, featuring dual-core RISC-V processors at 400MHz with AI instruction extensions. Designed for rich HMI applications, it includes MIPI interfaces for camera and display, H.264 encoding, and extensive peripheral support. Typically paired with ESP32-C6 for wireless connectivity.

specifications.Processor: Dual-core RISC-V, up to 400 MHz + LP-Core at 40 MHz
specifications.Connectivity: Ethernet, paired with ESP32-C6 for WiFi 6/BT via SDIO
specifications.Communication: Multiple UART, SPI, I2C, SDIO Host 3.0
specifications.Security: Secure Boot, Flash Encryption, Key Management Unit

codeSnippet.title: Dual-Core Processing Example

boards.name: ESP32-S2 DevKit
boards.shortDesc: Cost-effective ESP32 with USB Native, WiFi 4, ideal for USB devices and IoT sensors
boards.fullDescription: The ESP32-S2 is Espressif's cost-effective WiFi-only microcontroller featuring a single-core Xtensa LX7 processor at 240MHz with native USB support. Designed for IoT applications where Bluetooth isn't required, it offers excellent value with built-in USB OTG, making it perfect for USB HID devices and WiFi sensors.

specifications.Processor: 32-bit Xtensa LX7 single-core, up to 240 MHz
specifications.USB: USB OTG 1.1 (native, no USB-to-Serial chip needed)

codeSnippet.title: USB Device Example

boards.name: ESP32-WROVER Development Board
boards.shortDesc: ESP32 with 8MB PSRAM for memory-intensive applications like camera and AI processing
boards.fullDescription: The ESP32-WROVER module features an additional 8MB of PSRAM (Pseudo Static RAM), making it ideal for applications requiring large memory buffers such as camera image processing, audio streaming, and AI inference. Perfect for advanced projects that need more memory than standard ESP32 boards.

specifications.Processor: Dual-core Xtensa LX6, up to 240 MHz

codeSnippet.title: PSRAM Usage Example

boards.name: M5Stack Core2
boards.shortDesc: All-in-one ESP32 development kit with 2" touch screen, speaker, microphone, and IMU
boards.fullDescription: The M5Stack Core2 is a comprehensive IoT development kit featuring an ESP32 with 16MB flash, 8MB PSRAM, a 320x240 capacitive touchscreen, stereo speakers, microphone, 6-axis IMU, vibration motor, RTC, and SD card slot. Everything you need in one compact, stackable package with Grove connectors for easy expansion.

specifications.Audio: 1W stereo speakers, NS4168 amplifier, I2S microphone

pinout.M-Bus: Full GPIO expansion via stackable modules

codeSnippet.title: M5Stack Touch Display Example

boards.name: M5StickC Plus
boards.shortDesc: Pocket-sized ESP32 with 1.14" color display, IMU, microphone, and battery
boards.fullDescription: The M5StickC Plus is an ultra-compact ESP32 development kit featuring a 1.14-inch color LCD, 6-axis IMU, infrared transmitter, microphone, buzzer, and 120mAh battery in a tiny wearable form factor. Perfect for portable IoT devices, wearables, and quick prototyping projects.

codeSnippet.title: M5StickC Display Example

boards.name: LILYGO T-Display ESP32
boards.shortDesc: ESP32 with built-in 1.14" IPS color display and two programmable buttons
boards.fullDescription: The LILYGO T-Display features an ESP32 with a built-in 1.14-inch 135x240 IPS color display, two programmable buttons, and battery charging circuit. Its compact design and integrated display make it perfect for creating portable displays, gauges, and interactive devices without external components.

specifications.GPIO: Exposed I/O pins on both sides

codeSnippet.title: T-Display Hello World

boards.name: ESP32-Ethernet-Kit
boards.shortDesc: ESP32 development board with wired Ethernet, PoE support, and CAN bus
boards.fullDescription: The ESP32-Ethernet-Kit combines WiFi/Bluetooth with wired 100Mbps Ethernet via the IP101GRI PHY chip. Features optional PoE (Power over Ethernet), CAN bus interface, SD card slot, and LCD interface, making it ideal for industrial applications requiring reliable wired connectivity.

codeSnippet.title: Ethernet Connection Example

boards.name: Adafruit HUZZAH32 ESP32 Feather
boards.shortDesc: Feather-format ESP32 with LiPo charging, Feather Wing compatible
boards.fullDescription: The Adafruit HUZZAH32 brings ESP32 power to the Feather ecosystem, featuring automatic LiPo battery charging, JST connector, and compatibility with all Adafruit Feather Wings. Perfect for portable WiFi/Bluetooth projects with easy battery management and modular expansion.

pinout.GPIO: Multiple GPIO with ADC, PWM, I2C, SPI

codeSnippet.title: Battery Monitor Example

compatibleBoards: All Adafruit Feather Wings and accessories

boards.name: TTGO LoRa32 V2 (ESP32 + LoRa + OLED)
boards.shortDesc: ESP32 with LoRa SX1276, OLED display, and WiFi/BT for long-range IoT
boards.fullDescription: The TTGO LoRa32 V2 combines ESP32's WiFi/Bluetooth with Semtech SX1276 LoRa transceiver (868/915MHz) and a 0.96-inch OLED display. Perfect for long-range wireless communication projects spanning several kilometers, ideal for sensor networks, tracking, and remote monitoring in areas without cellular coverage.

pinout.LoRa: SPI (MOSI, MISO, SCK, CS, RST, DIO)

codeSnippet.title: LoRa Send Example
